questions.q: What was the main task of the Constituent Assembly of India?
options: To frame the Constitution of India
questions.explanation: The Constituent Assembly was convened to draft and adopt the Constitution of independent India.

questions.q: Who presided over the Constituent Assembly as its permanent President?
questions.explanation: Dr. Rajendra Prasad was elected the permanent President of the Constituent Assembly.

questions.q: Who chaired the Drafting Committee of the Constituent Assembly?
questions.explanation: Dr. B. R. Ambedkar chaired the Drafting Committee and is regarded as the chief architect of the Constitution.

questions.q: In which month and year did the Constituent Assembly first meet?
questions.explanation: The Constituent Assembly held its first sitting in December 1946.

questions.q: Who moved the famous Objectives Resolution in the Constituent Assembly?
questions.explanation: Jawaharlal Nehru moved the Objectives Resolution in December 1946, which laid down the guiding principles of the Constitution.

questions.q: Roughly how long did the Constituent Assembly take to complete the Constitution?
options: About two years and eleven months
questions.explanation: The Assembly took about two years, eleven months, and eighteen days to draft and adopt the Constitution.

questions.q: The Constituent Assembly was set up based on the recommendations of which 1946 mission?
questions.explanation: The Constituent Assembly was constituted under the framework proposed by the Cabinet Mission of 1946.

questions.q: Who served as the temporary or interim chairman at the very first meeting of the Constituent Assembly?
questions.explanation: Dr. Sachchidananda Sinha, the oldest member, was the temporary chairman at the inaugural session in December 1946.

questions.q: The Objectives Resolution moved by Nehru later formed the basis of which part of the Constitution?
questions.explanation: The Objectives Resolution was substantially incorporated into the Preamble of the Constitution.

questions.q: On what date did the Constituent Assembly finally adopt the Constitution?
questions.explanation: The Constituent Assembly adopted the Constitution on 26 November 1949, now observed as Constitution Day.

questions.q: Members of the Constituent Assembly were chosen mainly through:
options: Indirect election by the provincial legislative assemblies
questions.explanation: Members were largely elected indirectly by the elected members of the provincial assemblies, with seats also for the princely states.

questions.q: Who served as the constitutional adviser to the Constituent Assembly and prepared an initial draft?
questions.explanation: Sir B. N. Rau was the constitutional adviser who prepared an initial draft for the Drafting Committee to consider.

questions.q: After Partition, the membership of the Constituent Assembly was reduced to roughly how many?
questions.explanation: Following Partition the Assembly's strength was around 299 members for the Indian Union.

questions.q: The Drafting Committee chaired by Ambedkar consisted of how many members in total?
questions.explanation: The Drafting Committee had seven members, with Ambedkar as its chairman.

questions.q: Which woman member, a noted social worker, served in the Constituent Assembly and on its committees?
questions.explanation: Hansa Mehta was among the women members of the Constituent Assembly and argued for equal rights for women.

questions.q: The Constituent Assembly also functioned as which body until the first general elections?
questions.explanation: After 1947 the Assembly doubled as the provisional Parliament of the Dominion of India until elections were held.

questions.q: Which leader chaired the important Union Powers Committee and the Union Constitution Committee?
questions.explanation: Nehru chaired key committees including the Union Powers Committee and the Union Constitution Committee.

questions.q: Sardar Patel chaired which crucial committees dealing with rights and minorities in the Assembly?
options: The Advisory Committee on Fundamental Rights and Minorities
questions.explanation: Patel chaired the Advisory Committee on Fundamental Rights, Minorities and Tribal Areas.

questions.q: The phrase 'We, the People of India' that opens the Constitution reflects the spirit of which Assembly resolution?
questions.explanation: Nehru's Objectives Resolution emphasized the sovereignty of the people, echoed in the Preamble's opening words.

questions.q: Members nominated by the Muslim League largely did what after Partition was decided?
options: Withdrew to form Pakistan's separate assembly
questions.explanation: Most Muslim League members left the Indian Constituent Assembly to take part in Pakistan's own constituent body.

questions.q: Which scholar-statesman who later became Education Minister was a member of the Constituent Assembly?
questions.explanation: Maulana Azad, a senior Congress leader and India's first Education Minister, was a member of the Assembly.

questions.q: In total, how many sessions did the Constituent Assembly hold while framing the Constitution?
questions.explanation: The Constituent Assembly met in eleven sessions over the period of drafting the Constitution.

questions.q: The Objectives Resolution was moved by Nehru on which date in 1946?
questions.explanation: Nehru introduced the Objectives Resolution on 13 December 1946, days after the Assembly first convened on 9 December.

questions.q: Approximately how many amendments were considered during the clause-by-clause debate on the draft Constitution?
questions.explanation: The Assembly considered roughly two thousand amendments during its detailed reading of the draft Constitution.

questions.q: Which member, a distinguished jurist from Madras, was a key figure on the Drafting Committee alongside Ambedkar?
questions.explanation: Alladi Krishnaswami Ayyar, an eminent lawyer, was an influential member of the Drafting Committee.

questions.q: The signing of the completed Constitution by members took place primarily on which date?
questions.explanation: Members signed the Constitution on 24 January 1950, two days before it came into force on 26 January 1950.

questions.q: Who served as Vice-President of the Constituent Assembly, assisting Rajendra Prasad?
questions.explanation: Harendra Coomar Mukherjee served as the Vice-President (Vice-Chairman) of the Constituent Assembly.

questions.q: The original Constitution as adopted in 1949 contained roughly how many articles?
questions.explanation: The Constitution as originally adopted had 395 articles, 22 parts, and 8 schedules.

questions.q: The calligrapher who hand-wrote the original Constitution in flowing italic script was:
questions.explanation: Prem Behari Narain Raizada hand-wrote the original Constitution, while artists led by Nandalal Bose illustrated it.

questions.q: Which member, an Anglo-Indian leader, represented his community in the Constituent Assembly?
questions.explanation: Frank Anthony represented the Anglo-Indian community in the Constituent Assembly.
